channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: MPs vote against an outright social media ban for under-16s, favouring flexible regulation
item.description: MPs in the UK Parliament have voted against implementing an Australia-style blanket ban on social media use for under-16s, instead favouring flexible regulatory powers for ministers. The proposal had ...

item.title: Rebuilding Australia’s Public Service Requires More Than Cutting Outsourced Roles
item.description: The Albanese government’s move to reduce reliance on external contractors and consultants is a positive step for the Australian Public Service (APS), reversing a trend that had cost the Commonwealth $...

item.title: Public scepticism and challenges of decentralising police in Nigeria
item.description: For years, Nigerians don dey use the centralised police system as if na necessary evil, especially after the civil war, but e don clear say the system no dey work well for di people. The 2023 World In...

item.title: Vamsadhara Lift Irrigation Project Expected to Benefit Over One Lakh Acres by April
item.description: The Srikakulam district administration is making concerted efforts to complete the Vamsadhara Lift Irrigation Scheme (LIS) by April 2026. This project, located in Hiramandalam, aims to provide water t...

item.title: Pakistan Rules Out Mass Evacuation from Middle East Amid Regional Tensions
item.description: Senior officials from Pakistan's Ministry of Foreign Affairs briefed the National Assembly Standing Committee on Overseas Pakistanis, assuring that no mass evacuation of Pakistani nationals from the M...

item.title: Gatchalian: Online classes not feasible for public schools due to digital access issues amid rising fuel prices
item.description: Senator Sherwin Gatchalian, vice chairperson of the Senate committees on basic education and energy, stated on March 10, 2026, that shifting to online classes is not a practical solution for public sc...

item.title: Establishing the Ekurhuleni University of Applied Science and Innovation to Boost Regional Growth
item.description: The City of Ekurhuleni, a major economic and industrial hub in Gauteng, is poised to benefit significantly from the establishment of the Ekurhuleni University of Applied Science and Innovation. The un...

item.title: Realistic Assessment of Kenya's 10-Point Agenda Progress
item.description: The article evaluates the progress of Kenya's 10-point agenda, which President William Ruto launched amid public protests against high taxes and living costs in mid-2024. It gives a balanced scorecard...

item.title: Two Melville residents killed in head-on collision on Highway 1 near Balgonie after wrong-lane driving
item.description: A tragic collision on Highway 1 in Saskatchewan claimed the lives of two residents from Melville on Saturday, March 7, 2026. According to Saskatchewan RCMP, the incident occurred around 8:15 a.m. in t...

item.title: Quezon City authorities plan permanent relocation for families affected by Barangay Pinyahan fire
item.description: MANILA, Philippines — The Quezon City government is actively planning a permanent relocation program for thousands of families displaced by a major fire in Barangay Pinyahan along NIA Road last week. ...

item.title: Trump Comments Raise Questions About US Role in Iran School Bombing
item.description: The latest statements from former President Donald Trump have stirred significant controversy regarding the US role in the conflict in Iran. Analysts are pointing out inconsistencies in the White Hous...

item.title: US Warns of Potential Activation of Iranian Sleeper Cells Worldwide Amid Escalating Conflict
item.description: US officials have issued a warning that Iran may have activated sleeper cells around the world in response to escalating tensions with the United States and Israel. Following the death of Iran's Supre...

item.title: Kenya Airways and Flydubai Resume Key Flights Between East Africa and Dubai
item.description: Kenya Airways has officially resumed passenger flights to Dubai, becoming the first East African airline to reopen this important route after a few days of suspension caused by regional tensions. The ...

item.title: Standard Bank client loses over R4 million in alleged phishing scam
item.description: A Standard Bank client in Tshwane reportedly lost more than R4 million in a single day due to alleged cellphone banking fraud. The incident allegedly occurred while she was on the phone with the bank’...

item.title: UN Recognises Tanzania's Efforts to Restore Peace and Unity After 2025 Elections
item.description: Dar es Salaam – Tanzania has received international commendation from the United Nations for the efforts it has made to restore peace and stability following the disturbances that happened during and ...

item.title: Kenya Pipeline Shares Officially Begin Trading at NSE with President Ruto in Attendance
item.description: On Tuesday, March 10, 2026, President William Ruto officially presided over the bell-ringing ceremony marking the commencement of trading for Kenya Pipeline Company shares at the Nairobi Securities Ex...

item.title: Australian Government Offers Support to Iranian Women’s Soccer Team Seeking Asylum
item.description: Five members of the Iranian women's national soccer team have been granted humanitarian visas to remain in Australia after a tense escape from their handlers on the Gold Coast. The players, including ...

item.title: Surrey Police investigator Alex Bromley describes her work tracking child abusers on the dark web and the challenges of rising online threats
item.description: Alex Bromley, a civilian investigator with Surrey Police, is one of the few UK specialists dedicated to identifying paedophiles on the dark web. Her work involves scrutinising minute details in videos...

item.title: Ramazan Fosters Harmony Between Hindus and Muslims in Mithi, Sindh
item.description: In Mithi, a town in Sindh with a majority Hindu population, Ramazan has become more than just a religious observance; it has evolved into a symbol of interfaith solidarity. Many Hindus in the city als...

item.title: PDP Board of Trustees Meeting Scheduled by Wike Faction After Court Ruling
